'''Scizzic''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Ugnaught/Legends|Ugnaught]] [[Technician/Legends|mechanic]] who worked for [[Talandro Starlyte]] on the [[Space station/Legends|space station]] [[Starlyte Station]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. A skilled mechanic, he was hired to be the primary technician for the station. However, he eventually branched out and converted a [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] into a repair shop called the [[Graveyard (station)|Graveyard]], where he performed [[Starship/Legends|starship]] repairs as well. He modified several ships including the ''[[Starbound Misfit]]'', which he altered as a favor to Starlyte for his girlfriend, [[Lynnori]].

==Biography==
Scizzic was an [[Ugnaught/Legends|Ugnaught]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] mechanical genius who in his youth preferred fixing [[Mining/Legends|mining]] machinery to using it. Some individuals believed that he met [[Talandro Starlyte]] in [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] over the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]]. Starlyte recognized his mechanical and technical aptitude and offered him an opportunity to repair [[Starlyte Station]], the [[Space station/Legends|space facility]] he had recently acquired from the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Scizzic lived up to Starlyte's expectations and had all of the important systems functioning in a very short [[Time/Legends|time]]. Once the station was operational, he set up shop in the [[Graveyard (station)|Graveyard]], the nickname for the main [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] bay of Starlyte Station, where he performed [[Starship/Legends|starship]] repairs.<ref name="WHSV" />

The Graveyard quickly became littered with a large variety of ships and their parts, which Scizzic would use during repairs. He insisted on doing all of his repair work personally, with some assistance from a few older [[Maintenance droid/Legends|repair droids]]. While his services were significantly more expensive than those of most other repair shops, some people believed the results were very reliable and worth the extra cost. He tinkered with ships as he repaired them and would sometimes add unrequested features in the process. Scizzic also created original improvements, such as a more efficient configuration for [[Power coupling/Legends|power couplings]]. When Starlyte purchased the ''[[Starbound Misfit]]'' for his girlfriend, [[Lynnori]], Starlyte had Scizzic modify the ship. His repair operation became highly profitable for him despite the remote location of the station and the overall appearance of the Graveyard, strewn with scrap and derelict ships. In addition to his work there, he continued to perform minor repairs on Starlyte Station during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]].<ref name="WHSV" />

==Personality and traits==
Unlike other Ugnaughts, Scizzic had no interest in mining. Rather than make use of the machinery, he preferred to fix it. Due to his mechanical talents and love for tinkering and fixing things, Scizzic made a career out of starship repair. He was a bit shy and reserved, although he could become very excitable, especially when he worked on a ship. Despite the profitability of his operation, he continued to perform the repairs himself, as he enjoyed his work. When he would make a new discovery, his chattering would reach manic levels. Scizzic was not interested in conversation about subjects other than starship repair, as the only thing he loved more than fixing ships was talking about fixing them.<ref name="WHSV" />

==Skills and abilities==
A genius [[Technician/Legends|mechanic]], Scizzic was very skilled in all matters of repair, including [[Droid/Legends|droids]], [[Computer/Legends|computers]], machinery [[Engineer/Legends|engineering]], [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] vehicles, [[Transport/Legends|space transports]], [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] and starship weaponry as well as programming droids and computers. He also had rudimentary skill as a hover-vehicle [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] and had a keen [[Eye/Legends|eye]] for the discovery of valuable items that had been discarded as junk. He could handle a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] if necessary, but he was more likely to hide amongst the stacks of ship [[Hull/Legends|hulls]] and spare parts than engage in a dedicated firefight.<ref name="WHSV" />

==Equipment==
Scizzic carried around a set of tools, which at any given time might include [[Hydrospanner/Legends|hydrospanners]], a [[plasma welder]], a diagnostic scanner, a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]], a [[Laser cutter/Legends|laser cutter]], and various [[repair kit]]s. He also had a concealed [[Hold-out blaster/Legends|hold-out blaster]].<ref name="WHSV" />

==Behind the scenes==
Scizzic was designed by [[Paul Danner]] and [[Bill Smith]] for ''[[Wretched Hives of Scum & Villainy]]'', a supplement for the ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' that was published by [[West End Games]] in [[1997]]. The book included illustrations of Scizzic that were created by [[Joe Corroney]].
